evidence_quotes.quote: Today, the "ownership" of a game that a consumer may have, can be revoked or restricted by the publisher without refund or compensation.

I ask that you stop publishers from freely revoking ownership/license rights from the consumer when fully purchased (e.g. allowing for ownership even after support goes down and not requesting complete destruction and deletion of all copies; Making physical copies actually physical discs and not just an elaborate download key).

paragraphs.text: Hello dear European Comission. I am but a humble civillian of this beautiful continent, and come to you with quite the pressing matter today. Today, the digital space is more importaant than ever, in all of it's aspects. Many pieces of media are purchased, however, an issue that really is beginning to plague the video game industry, is that of ownership. Today, the "ownership" of a game that a consumer may have, can be revoked or restricted by the publisher without refund or compensation. This can lead to (and already has led to) frankly predatory business models. I understand that you all are immensly busy people, however, as a citizen, I cannot live comfortably with this idea, hence why I write this feedback. I ask that you stop publishers from freely revoking ownership/license rights from the consumer when fully purchased (e.g. allowing for ownership even after support goes down and not requesting complete destruction and deletion of all copies; Making physical copies actually physical discs and not just an elaborate download key). In conclusion, me, and thousands of EU citizens would be extremely thankful if you could begin to protect game ownership, as it's very important to us. Thank you for reading this and please consider.
